TO JOSEPH TO BUY CORN. The clause is inverted97It literally reads: to buy corn to Joseph. and must be understood as: And all the countries came into Egypt to Joseph to buy corn.

Ys ead there was'a great cry of tho people and of their wives against their brethren the Jews.
And Pharoh was angry when he heard concerning his two servants, the chief cup--bearer and the chief baker.

Because he heard that they had regrets. Rashi is answering the question: Why did he cry? He should have been angry with them, as he heard that they themselves admitted that they did wrong. Therefore Rashi explains that he cried because he heard that they had regrets. (Maharshal)
And Pharoh was angry when he heard concerning his two servants, the chief cup--bearer and the chief baker.
And heard the words. That Eliezer had asked for lodgings.

Because he heard that they had regrets. Rashi is answering the question: Why did he cry? He should have been angry with them, as he heard that they themselves admitted that they did wrong. Therefore Rashi explains that he cried because he heard that they had regrets. (Maharshal)
He heard the words. He heard that Lavan’s sons were slandering him out of jealousy.

Ye exact usury every one of his brother; which was against the plain and positive law of God, #De 23:19,20; especially in this time of public calamity and dearth. (Poole's Commentary)

We after our ability have redeemed our brethren the Jews, which were sold unto the heathen: I, and my, brethren, and predecessors, have used our utmost interest and power, both with the kings of Persia, that our brethren might be redeemed from that bondage into which God had sold them for their sins; and with particular persons in Babylon and Persia, and the adjacent parts, whose bond-slaves the…

Smith and Sjodahl gave further enlightenment on the subject of shaking the dust off one's feet: "The significance of this solemn act is made clear in Nehemiah 5:13. This prophet, after having taken a promise of the priests, shook his lap and said, 'God shake out every man from his house, and from his labor, that performeth not this promise, even thus be he shaken out and empty.' To shake the dust…

the former governors...had taken...bread and wine, besides forty shekels of silver—The income of Eastern governors is paid partly in produce, partly in money. "Bread" means all sorts of provision. (JFB Bible Commentary)

3 (return) [ It may not be amiss to compare the daily furniture of king
Solomon's table, here set down, and 1 Kings 4;22, 23, with the like
daily furniture of Nehemiah the governor's table, after the Jews were
come back from Babylon; and to remember withal, that Nehemiah was now
building the walls of Jerusalem, and maintained, more than usual, above
a hundred and fifty considerable men every day,…
